text:
Market today: AI demand narrative is carrying tech into month-end with oil as the clear loser. Headline 10 spells it out - AI spend is keeping growth names bid while energy rolls over. That's your environment: tailwind for chips/tech, rotation out of cyclicals. Macro wildcard is yen/Fed tension (Japan headline) - if dollar volatility spikes, growth names can gap ugly fast.

Your holdings: Everything is green and above stops - no fires today. SOXL (+4.8%) is a 3x leveraged chip ETF - it decays over time and moves violently; on margin that's a double-amplifier, so your $200 stop must be respected hard. SPAL is brand-new, very volatile, still finding its range - tiny position or it can evaporate on you.

Watch: (1) NVDA price action - it's the engine of SMH/SOXL; any crack there ripples through your whole book. (2) Dollar/yen - if Japan intervention fails and yen spikes, risk-off can hit fast.

Bottom line: Book is in good shape but leveraged names on margin demand tight stops - protect what you have.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is your hard line - on margin + 3x leverage a gap down hits twice as hard. Do not move it up; just honor it.
SPAL: Brand-new leveraged ETF, very thin cushion above $21.38 stop - keep size small or tighten stop given margin exposure.
No new buys today - scanner setups (NVDA 7/12 grade C, ALAB in daily premium/chasing) lack a strong enough catalyst to justify adding margin risk right now.
